name: Rhinocryptidae
description: Tapaculos are secretive, ground-dwelling passerines of South and Central America, known for their loud calls and elusive behavior. Their name comes from the Spanish 'tapaculo' meaning 'cover your rear'. Many species have restricted ranges in the Andes.

children.name: Scytalopus
children.description: Scytalopus is the most speciose genus of tapaculos, containing many small, dark species found primarily in the Andes.

children.name: Scytalopus magellanicus
children.description: The Magellanic tapaculo is a small, dark grey bird found in southern South America from Chile to Tierra del Fuego. It inhabits dense undergrowth in Nothofagus forests and is more often heard than seen.

children.name: Scytalopus latrans
children.description: The blackish tapaculo is a sooty-grey bird found in the Andes from Colombia to Peru. It favors humid montane forest understory, where its repetitive song echoes through the thickets.

children.name: Scytalopus latebricola
children.description: The brown-rumped tapaculo is a dark grey bird with a distinctive brown rump and lower back, endemic to the Santa Marta Mountains of Colombia. It inhabits the understory of humid montane forests. Its song is a series of fast, rattling notes.

children.name: Scytalopus caracae
children.description: The Caracas tapaculo is a small, dark grey tapaculo with brownish flanks, endemic to the Coastal Range of northern Venezuela. It inhabits humid montane forests near Caracas. Its song is a fast series of high-pitched notes.

children.name: Scytalopus atratus
children.description: The northern white-crowned tapaculo is a dark grey bird with a distinctive white crown patch, found in the Andes from Colombia to Peru. It inhabits the understory of cloud forests at mid to high elevations. Its song is a series of fast, metallic notes.

children.name: Scytalopus parvirostris
children.description: The trilling tapaculo is a small, dark grey tapaculo with a relatively short bill, found in the Andes of Peru and Bolivia. It inhabits humid montane forests and elfin woodlands. Its song is a long, fast trill that gives the species its name.

children.name: Scytalopus bolivianus
children.description: The Bolivian tapaculo is a dark grey bird with brownish flanks and a slightly paler throat, endemic to the Yungas of Bolivia and southern Peru. It inhabits the understory of humid montane forests. Its song is a slow series of musical notes.

children.name: Scytalopus spillmanni
children.description: A dark grey tapaculo from the Andes of Colombia and Ecuador.

children.name: Scytalopus fuscus
children.description: A dark tapaculo found in the Andes of Chile and Argentina.

children.name: Scytalopus senilis
children.description: A grey tapaculo with a paler nape, found in the Andes of Colombia.

children.name: Scytalopus acutirostris
children.description: A small dark tapaculo with a relatively long pointed bill from Peru.

children.name: Scytalopus albifrons
children.description: A dark tapaculo with white forehead, found in the Andes of Venezuela.

children.name: Scytalopus argentifrons
children.description: A small tapaculo with silvery-grey forehead, found in Costa Rica and Panama.

children.name: Scytalopus chocorum
children.description: A tapaculo of the Choco region in western Colombia and Ecuador.

children.name: Scytalopus canus
children.description: A high-altitude tapaculo found in paramo grasslands of Colombia.

children.name: Scytalopus getulae
children.description: A tapaculo of the coastal mountains of Brazil.

children.name: Scytalopus gonzalezi
children.description: A recently described tapaculo from the Pacific slope of the Andes in Colombia and Ecuador.

children.name: Scytalopus griseicollis
children.description: A grey tapaculo with pale belly, found in the Andes of Colombia and Venezuela.

children.name: Scytalopus macropus
children.description: A large tapaculo with notably large feet from the Andes of Peru.

children.name: Scytalopus merops
children.description: A tapaculo endemic to the Merida region of Venezuela.

children.name: Scytalopus micropterus
children.description: A tapaculo with a relatively long tail from the Andes of Colombia and Ecuador.

children.name: Scytalopus perijanus
children.description: A tapaculo of the Perija Mountains on the Colombia-Venezuela border.

children.name: Scytalopus plumbiceps
children.description: A lead-grey tapaculo found in the Andes of Ecuador and Peru.

children.name: Scytalopus robbinsi
children.description: A recently discovered tapaculo from the Pacific slope of Ecuador.

children.name: Scytalopus sanctaepaulae
children.description: A tapaculo endemic to the Santa Marta Mountains of Colombia.

children.name: Scytalopus simonsi
children.description: A dark-grey tapaculo from the high Andes of Bolivia and Peru.

children.name: Scytalopus steini
children.description: A tapaculo of the high Andes of Bolivia and Peru.

children.name: Scytalopus stolzmanni
children.description: A tapaculo found in the high Andes of Peru and Ecuador.

children.name: Scytalopus unicolor
children.description: A uniformly dark grey tapaculo from the Andes of Peru.

children.name: Scytalopus urubambae
children.description: A tapaculo restricted to the Urubamba Valley in southern Peru.

children.name: Scytalopus variabilis
children.description: A highly variable tapaculo found in the Andes of Peru and Bolivia.

children.name: Scytalopus vicinarior
children.description: A recently split tapaculo from the Atlantic Forest of Brazil.

children.name: Scytalopus zaroensis
children.description: A recently discovered tapaculo from the Zaro region of Peru.

children.name: Scytalopus schulteni
children.description: A tapaculo from the Peruvian Andes.

children.name: Scytalopus krabbei
children.description: A tapaculo from the Cordillera Azul, Peru.

children.name: Scytalopus antioquiae
children.description: A tapaculo from Antioquia, Colombia.

children.name: Scytalopus altifrontis
children.description: A high-altitude tapaculo from the central Andes.

children.name: Scytalopus transandeanus
children.description: A tapaculo from the Pacific slope of Ecuador.

children.name: Liosceles
children.description: Liosceles is a monotypic genus of tapaculo with a distinctive white crown stripe.

children.name: Liosceles thoracicus
children.description: The white-crested tapaculo has a bold white eyebrow and throat contrasting with dark brown upperparts. It inhabits lowland Amazonian forests and is one of the few tapaculos found at low elevations.

children.name: Pteroptochos
children.description: Pteroptochos is a genus of large tapaculos endemic to Chile and adjacent Argentina, known for their loud, far-carrying calls.

children.name: Pteroptochos castaneus
children.description: The chestnut-throated huet-huet is a large, striking tapaculo with a rich chestnut throat and breast. It is endemic to the temperate forests of southern Chile and is named for its loud, repetitive call.

children.name: Pteroptochos tarnii
children.description: A large tapaculo with black throat and chestnut underparts, found in southern Chile and Argentina.

children.name: Scelorchilus
children.description: Scelorchilus is a genus of tapaculo with two species endemic to southern South America, known for their loud calls.

children.name: Scelorchilus rubecula
children.description: The chucao tapaculo is a colorful, robin-like bird of Chilean and Argentine temperate forests. It is named for its loud, distinctive song and holds cultural significance in Mapuche folklore.

children.name: Scelorchilus albicollis
children.description: A colourful tapaculo with white throat and chestnut belly, endemic to Chile.

children.name: Myornis
children.description: A monotypic genus of tapaculo with uniform grey plumage, found at high elevations in the Andes. It is adapted to the harsh conditions of the paramo and puna grasslands.

children.name: Myornis senilis
children.description: The ash-colored tapaculo is a uniform slate-grey bird with a slightly paler belly, found at high elevations in the Andes from Colombia to Peru. It inhabits paramo grasslands and elfin forests, hopping through dense vegetation. Its song is a series of rapid, high-pitched notes.

children.name: Acropternis
children.description: A monotypic genus containing the largest and most spectacularly patterned tapaculo. It is named for the distinctive ocellated (eye-like) spots on its underparts.

children.name: Acropternis orthonyx
children.description: The ocellated tapaculo is the largest tapaculo, with striking black plumage covered in white ocellated spots on the underparts and a chestnut crown. It is found in the cloud forests of the Andes from Colombia to Peru. Its powerful, musical song is a series of clear, fluty whistles.

children.name: Eleoscytalopus
children.description: A genus of small, distinctive tapaculos endemic to the Atlantic Forest of Brazil. They have relatively long legs and are among the most colourful of the otherwise drab tapaculos.

children.name: Eleoscytalopus indigoticus
children.description: The white-breasted tapaculo has slate-blue upperparts, a white throat and breast, and a distinctive rusty vent. It is endemic to the Atlantic Forest of southeastern Brazil. It inhabits the understory of humid forests and is more often heard than seen.

children.name: Eleoscytalopus psychopompus
children.description: The Bahia tapaculo is a small, dark blue-grey tapaculo with a white belly, endemic to the state of Bahia in eastern Brazil. It inhabits lowland Atlantic Forest and is classified as endangered due to severe habitat loss. Very little is known about its ecology.

children.name: Merulaxis
children.description: A genus of tapaculos with distinctive stiff, bristle-like feathers at the base of the bill, from which they get their common name. They are endemic to the Atlantic Forest of Brazil.

children.name: Merulaxis ater
children.description: The slaty bristlefront is a dark slate-grey bird with a prominent tuft of bristles at the base of its bill and a rufous vent, found in the Atlantic Forest of southeastern Brazil. It inhabits the dense understory of humid lowland forests. Its song is a series of clear, musical notes.

children.name: Merulaxis stresemanni
children.description: Stresemann's bristlefront is a critically endangered species of bird in the family Rhinocryptidae, commonly known as tapaculos. It is endemic to Brazil, where there are 50 or fewer individuals living exclusively in the Mata do Passarinho Reserve.
